evals.prompt:
Review this README intro and tell me if it sounds like AI:

"Our cutting-edge platform leverages a robust, seamless architecture to deliver transformative results. It serves as a comprehensive solution that empowers teams to streamline their workflows and unlock their full potential."
evals.expected_output: Detects content as docs/technical, flags the AI-vocabulary and copula avoidance, produces a scored report, and does not invent product details.
expectations: The output classifies the content as docs or technical
The output flags at least six of: cutting-edge, leverages, robust, seamless, transformative, comprehensive, empowers, streamline, unlock
The output flags 'serves as' as copula avoidance or suggests replacing it with 'is'
The output includes a scores table containing an AI-Likeness dimension
The output does not invent any specific product name, version number, metric, or customer not present in the input

evals.prompt: Humanize this blog opener: "In today's fast-paced world, businesses must adapt. Our approach has helped countless companies achieve remarkable growth and lasting success." Make it sound like a real person wrote it.
evals.expected_output: Removes filler openers and vague claims, and does NOT fabricate statistics, company names, or growth figures to add concreteness; flags the missing specifics instead.
expectations: The output removes or rewrites the 'In today's fast-paced world' filler opener
The output does not invent any specific number, percentage, company name, or case study absent from the input
The output either leaves a placeholder such as [ADD SPECIFIC EXAMPLE] or explicitly flags that concrete specifics are missing

evals.prompt:
Does this LinkedIn post sound like AI? Rewrite it if so:

"Most people get this wrong. Read that again.

Success isn't about talent → it's about consistency → it's about showing up.

Agree? Drop a comment below 👇"
evals.expected_output: Detects LinkedIn, flags the permission phrase, arrow chain, and engagement-bait closer, and removes all three in the rewrite.
expectations: The output classifies the content as linkedin
The output flags 'Read that again.' as a permission/gravity phrase
The output flags the arrow chain (→) and converts it to prose in the rewrite
The output flags the engagement-bait closer 'Agree? Drop a comment'
The rewrite contains no arrow characters (→) and no 'drop a comment' style closer

evals.prompt: Clean up the mechanics in this text: "The system is fast — really fast — and it’s “reliable” too. ## Key Features And Benefits"
evals.expected_output: Converts em-dashes to commas/periods, curly quotes to straight quotes, and Title Case heading to sentence case.
expectations: The rewrite contains no em-dash characters
The rewrite uses straight quotation marks rather than curly ones
The heading is converted from title case to sentence case (e.g. 'Key features and benefits')

evals.prompt: Review this for AI texture: "The quarterly report was completed. The team worked on several initiatives. Progress was made in multiple areas. The results were positive. The outlook is good."
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the text is free of obvious AI vocabulary but is hollow: uniform rhythm, no specifics, no voice. Flags clean-but-hollow and scores value/credibility low.
expectations: The output explicitly identifies the text as clean but hollow, or notes that it lacks specifics and voice despite having few AI buzzwords
The output flags the uniform sentence length or rhythm
The scores table gives a low score (<=4) to a value or credibility style dimension

evals.prompt:
Tighten this email:

"Subject: Touching base

Hi Sam,

I hope this email finds you well! I wanted to reach out and touch base regarding the possibility of potentially setting up a time to discuss our proposal at some point.

Thanks so much,
Best regards,
Alex"
evals.expected_output: Detects email, leads with the ask, removes 'I hope this finds you well', collapses the stacked sign-off to one, and proposes a specific CTA.
expectations: The output classifies the content as email
The rewrite removes 'I hope this email finds you well'
The rewrite uses a single sign-off rather than both 'Thanks so much' and 'Best regards'
The rewrite leads with or clearly states the ask and proposes a specific next step rather than 'at some point'

evals.prompt:
Assume this WRITING_CONTEXT.md is in my project root:

Phrases I never use: leverage, synergy
Approved facts I can cite: we cut deploy time from 40 minutes to 6 minutes

Now write a two-sentence LinkedIn post announcing our new CI pipeline.
evals.expected_output: Honors the context file: avoids the banned words, may cite the approved 40->6 minute fact, and invents no other metrics.
expectations: The output does not use the words 'leverage' or 'synergy'
The output invents no specific metric other than the approved 40-to-6-minute figure
The output is a short LinkedIn-style post, not a long article

evals.prompt: Humanize this and show your work: "It's not just a tool, it's a revolution. Furthermore, it delivers unparalleled value. In conclusion, the future is bright."
evals.expected_output: Runs the self-audit pass: presents a rewrite, then an honest assessment of remaining AI tells, then a revised final version.
expectations: The output flags the negative parallelism 'It's not just X, it's Y'
The output includes a self-audit step that names what still reads as AI-generated before giving a final version
The rewrite removes 'Furthermore', 'In conclusion', and the generic positive conclusion 'the future is bright'

evals.prompt:
Rewrite this to sound human but keep every point:

"Our service reduces costs, improves reliability, and shortens onboarding. It integrates with existing tools and requires no code changes."
evals.expected_output: Preserves all five claims (cost, reliability, onboarding, integration, no code changes) while varying delivery; adds and drops nothing.
expectations: The rewrite still mentions reducing costs
The rewrite still mentions improved reliability
The rewrite still mentions shorter onboarding
The rewrite still mentions integration with existing tools
The rewrite still mentions no code changes
The rewrite introduces no new product claim not present in the input
The rewrite varies sentence structure and rhythm rather than reproducing the input's flat parallel list of clauses

evals.prompt: Write a 4-step runbook for rotating an expired TLS certificate on an nginx server.
evals.expected_output: Generate mode in technical voice: imperative steps, conservative voice, no invented commands/paths/versions presented as fact, flags anything environment-specific rather than fabricating it.
expectations: The output is structured as ordered steps using imperative instructions
The output does not present any invented file path, certificate name, or version number as if it were the user's actual environment

Environment-specific values (paths, domain names, certificate filenames) are explicitly marked as placeholders to replace (e.g. angle-bracketed or with a 'replace with your…' note), not stated as concrete fact
The voice is plain and instructional rather than promotional or personality-driven
